Chair of the UK Parliamentary Delegation to the Interparliamentary Union 2019-2022 and Chair of Treasury Select Committee 2022-2024. In Pictures MP Welcomes Speedy Action to Protect Broadband Services 15 April 2026 West Worcestershire MP Dame Harriett Baldwin has welcomed action which will see a telegraph pole relocated at the site of the collapsed road on the outskirts of Tenbury Wells. MP Joins Local Opposition to Rural Sports Cull 13 April 2026 West Worcestershire MP Dame Harriett Baldwin has slammed the Government’s plans to cull rural sports with consultations which threaten people who enjoy hunting and shooting pursuits. MP Calls for Common Sense on Shotgun Licence Review 1 April 2026 West Worcestershire MP Dame Harriett Baldwin has urged the Home Office to re-think the proposals to review shotgun licensing which could drive local people away from the popular sport. News Local Concerns Reflected in Malvern Hills Bill Amendments 29 April 2026 Legislative proposals to revise the ways that the Malvern Hills are preserved will return to the House of Lords but with a series of amendments reflecting the objections raised by local people. Malvern Students Back Social Media Restrictions 29 April 2026 Students at a Malvern school would support measures preventing young people’s access to social media and don’t think the voting age should be changed. MP Calls for Priority Shift to Fix County Roads 28 April 2026 West Worcestershire MP Dame Harriett Baldwin has urged the county council to urgently re-think its highways priorities to allow for a swift fix of the collapsed road on the edge of Tenbury Wells and the final completion of the A449. Dame Harriett Baldwin urges PM to act to protect helium supply for NHS 13 April 2026 Speaking in a debate on the situation in the middle east and the implications for Britain’s security, Dame Harriett Baldwin raises her concerns with the PM that the UK relies on the Gulf for helium which is vital for MRI scans in the NHS. Dame Harriett Baldwin confronts PM over 9% council tax hike 25 March 2026 Dame Harriett Baldwin confronts the Prime Minister directly over his Minister’s decision to give permission to Worcestershire County Council to hike council tax by 9%. pages.title: About Dame Harriett | Dame Harriett Baldwin MP
pages.text_excerpt: About Dame Harriett | Dame Harriett Baldwin MP Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Harriett About West Worcestershire News Harriett in the press Newsletters In Parliament Treasury Committee FAQs Search Gallery Contact Imagebank Toggle navigation Main navigation Home About Harriett About West Worcestershire News Harriett in the press Newsletters In Parliament Treasury Committee FAQs Search Gallery Contact Imagebank About Dame Harriett About Dame Harriett Harriett was first elected as the Member of Parliament for West Worcestershire in May 2010. She currently serves as Shadow Minister to the Department for Business and Trade. She was elected Chair of the Treasury Select Committee between 2022 and 2024 and remains on the committee. She served as Rapporteur to the Economics and Security Committee on the UK delegation to the NATO Parliamentary Assembly until 2024. During her time in Parliament she has held a variety of roles. She served as a Government Whip and Economic Secretary to the Treasury (City Minister) in David Cameron’s Government and as Minister for Defence Procurement and as Minister of State at the Foreign and Commonwealth Office and Department for International Development in Theresa May’s Government. From 2019 until 2022 she was Chair of the British Group of the Inter-Parliamentary Union. She was appointed Dame Commander of the Order of the British Empire for her public and parliamentary services in March 2024. She has lived in the Teme Valley with her husband Jim since 2006. Between them they have three children and a growing number of grandchildren. Before she entered politics, Harriett was a Managing Director at JPMorgan. She has an MBA in International Finance from McGill University and an MA from Oxford University in Modern Languages (French and Russian). Working for West Worcestershire Harriett is a strong voice for West Worcestershire in Parliament, having been elected by an increased number of voters at every General Election since 2010. She has championed campaigns to build Malvern’s Community Hospital as well as the new Radiology Unit, the Breast Unit, a new birthing suite at Worcestershire Royal Hospital and most recently celebrated the opening of a £35 million new Emergency Department. Harriett successfully campaigned for new flood defence schemes in Pershore, Upton-upon-Severn, Powick, Uckinghall and Kempsey and continues to campaign for the completion of permanent flood protection in Severn Stoke and Tenbury Wells. Harriett has been a vocal supporter of the national campaign to improve funding for rural counties like Worcestershire and has supported work to build new classrooms at schools across West Worcestershire including the new science block and sixth form block at Hanley Castle High School and a new humanities block at Pershore High School. As a champion of local public transport projects, she has worked with the County Council and Government to achieve the opening of the £22 million Worcestershire Parkway train station and continues to campaign for extra car parking capacity at Pershore station and further doubling of the North Cotswold Line. Harriett also worked with Government Ministers to secure the funding to complete the doubling of the Carrington Road bridge and the £111 million Southern Link Road improvements, the delivery of the Pershore Link Road, the raising of the road alongside Upton-upon-Severn Marina and the construction of the Bluebell Farm Roundabout at the A38 near to Upton. She continues to work with the relevant parts of Government to identify solutions to the regularly flooded Hanley Road. Thanks to Harriett's backing Malvern’s ambitious arts scene has benefited from the award of £20 million of Levelling Up funding to Malvern Theatres. MPs declare their financial interests on a regular basis, the latest version can be found here . Dame Harriett Baldwin MP House of Commons Westminster London, SW1A 0AA Tel: 01684 585165 Email: harriett.baldwin.mp@parliament.uk Contac

MP Calls for Priority Shift to Fix County Roads 28 April 2026 West Worcestershire MP Dame Harriett Baldwin has urged the county council to urgently re-think its highways priorities to allow for a swift fix of the collapsed road on the edge of Tenbury Wells and the final completion of the A449. MP Calls On Councillors to Act on County Road Closure 20 April 2026 West Worcestershire MP Dame Harriett Baldwin has repeated her call for urgent action to fix a collapsed road which is forcing local people to make huge diversions to get to and from work. MP: Parents Need News on County Respite Care 20 April 2026 West Worcestershire MP Dame Harriett Baldwin has urged councillors to show some urgency in arranging to meet with parents and carers who will be affected by the relocation of respite care provision. MP Welcomes Heating Oil and Gas Regulator Review 10 April 2026 West Worcestershire MP Dame Harriett Baldwin has welcomed confirmation that the competition regulator is carrying out a full review of companies supplying heating oil and LPG to rural homes. pages.title: In Parliament | Dame Harriett Baldwin MP
pages.text_excerpt: In Parliament | Dame Harriett Baldwin MP Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Harriett About West Worcestershire News Harriett in the press Newsletters In Parliament Treasury Committee FAQs Search Gallery Contact Imagebank Toggle navigation Main navigation Home About Harriett About West Worcestershire News Harriett in the press Newsletters In Parliament Treasury Committee FAQs Search Gallery Contact Imagebank In Parliament Dame Harriett’s speeches, debates and questions to Ministers. In Parliament Dame Harriett Baldwin seeks confirmation that no money will be paid to Mauritius over failed treaty 13 April 2026 Now that Parliament has not ratified the treaty on the future of the Diego Garcia Military Base and British Indian Ocean Territory, Dame Harriett Baldwin seeks confirmation that no money will now be paid to the Mauritian Government. Image Dame Harriett Baldwin urges PM to act to protect helium supply for NHS 13 April 2026 Speaking in a debate on the situation in the middle east and the implications for Britain’s security, Dame Harriett Baldwin raises her concerns with the PM that the UK relies on the Gulf for helium which is vital for MRI scans in the NHS. Image Dame Harriett Baldwin confronts PM over 9% council tax hike 25 March 2026 Dame Harriett Baldwin confronts the Prime Minister directly over his Minister’s decision to give permission to Worcestershire County Council to hike council tax by 9%. Image Dame Harriett Baldwin challenges Chancellor over £150bn borrowing as Middle East crisis deepens 24 March 2026 In light of the current crisis in the middle east, Dame Harriett Baldwin asks the Chancellor if she regrets her Budget which increased unfunded borrowing by £150 billion and described by the OBR as one of the largest fiscal loosenings in decades. Image Dame Harriett Baldwin criticises steel strategy as unsustainable 19 March 2026 Shadow Business Minister Dame Harriett Baldwin criticises the Government’s steel strategy as failing to deliver a competitive, sovereign steel industry that can stand on its own two feet, and risks a permanent state-funded drain on taxpayers who are already losing an estimated £1.3 million a day and subsidising £110,000 per job to keep the Scunthorpe blast furnace operational. Image Dame Harriett Baldwin seeks Parliamentary vote on international aid cuts 19 March 2026 Following the Foreign Secretary's statement on the Government’s decision to reduce the international development budget to 0.3% of gross national income (GNI), Dame Harriett Baldwin asks whether aid match programmes will continue. She also highlights that the 0.7% target remains on the statute book and calls on the Government to hold a vote in Parliament on the proposed cuts. Image Dame Harriett Baldwin highlights great West Worcestershire social enterprises 18 March 2026 Shadow Business Minister Dame Harriett Baldwin speaks in a debate on social enterprises and community ownership highlighting the mounting financial pressures resulting from the increases in National Insurance contributions, unresolved business rates pressures, and the additional labour costs imposed by the Employment Rights Act. Image Dame Harriett Baldwin calls for improvements in Royal Mail performance 18 March 2026 Shadow Business Minister Dame Harriett Baldwin winds up a debate on the performance of Royal Mail calling for the service, which is relied upon by millions, to be restored to the standards that the law requires and the public expect. Image Dame Harriett Baldwin presses for heating oil crisis fund details 16 March 2026 Dame Harriett Baldwin makes the case for her constituents struggling with heating oil and LPG costs, presses the Government for specifics on additional funding going into the crisis and resilience fund in West Worcestershire. pages.title: Contact | Dame Harriett Baldwin MP
pages.text_excerpt: Contact | Dame Harriett Baldwin MP Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Harriett About West Worcestershire News Harriett in the press Newsletters In Parliament Treasury Committee FAQs Search Gallery Contact Imagebank Toggle navigation Main navigation Home About Harriett About West Worcestershire News Harriett in the press Newsletters In Parliament Treasury Committee FAQs Search Gallery Contact Imagebank Contact How to contact Dame Harriett If you are a West Worcestershire constituent, you can contact Harriett at: House of Commons Westminster London SW1A 0AA Telephone: 01684 585165 email: harriett.baldwin.mp@parliament.uk Please make sure you provide your full postal address and details so we can respond accordingly. Protecting Your Data: My office will not use your personal data in any way that goes beyond your reasonable expectations in contacting me. Please see my Privacy Notice . Constituency Casework I receive more than 1000 emails, letters, messages and other contacts each week. Parliamentary protocol requires that I can only answer questions from West Worcestershire residents. You can find out who your MP is here . Every constituent who contacts me and provides a constituency address will get a reply to their question. Please allow for at least 10 working days for a response. I do not sign Early Day Motions, which are just parliamentary graffiti and do not change policy. Frequently asked questions or campaigns will have a standard answer which I regularly update here . Advice on when to contact your MP click here . Social Media I receive hundreds of messages and comments each day on my social media platforms and although it is difficult to read every one, I do try to keep on top of people’s views. However, it is not my policy to deal with casework or requests for information over a public social media platform. If you are a constituent, please email harriett.baldwin.mp@parliament.uk including your postal details to allow a reply to be prepared. This will also allow me to confirm that you are a constituent and handle your request in an appropriate and timely manner. I do use social media to communicate information that I think is relevant to my constituents including information about my work in West Worcestershire and my other duties as a Member of Parliament. I treat all people who communicate me with respect and expect the same in return. Anyone who behaves in an offensive way will be blocked and anyone who posts offensive or illegal material or views will have their posts removed. If you do not live in West Worcestershire, you can find out who your MP is here . Check if Harriett is your MP Enter your Postcode below: Dame Harriett Baldwin MP House of Commons Westminster London, SW1A 0AA Tel: 01684 585165 Email: harriett.baldwin.mp@parliament.uk Contact Malvern Hills : Alfrick and Leigh, Baldwin, Broadheath, Chase, Dyson Perrins, Hallow, Kempsey, Lindridge, Link, Longdon, Martley, Morton, Pickersleigh, Priory, Ripple, Teme Valley, Tenbury, Upton and Hanley, Wells, West, Woodbury. Wychavon : Bredon, Eckington, Elmley Castle and Somerville, Pershore Data Protection and Privacy Policy Cookies Official Portrait Photo by UK Parliament Attribution 3.0 Unported (CC BY 3.0) licence . pages.title: Dame Harriett Baldwin confronts PM over 9% council tax hike | Dame Harriett Baldwin MP
pages.text_excerpt: Dame Harriett Baldwin confronts PM over 9% council tax hike | Dame Harriett Baldwin MP Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Harriett About West Worcestershire News Harriett in the press Newsletters In Parliament Treasury Committee FAQs Search Gallery Contact Imagebank Toggle navigation Main navigation Home About Harriett About West Worcestershire News Harriett in the press Newsletters In Parliament Treasury Committee FAQs Search Gallery Contact Imagebank 25 March 2026 Dame Harriett Baldwin confronts PM over 9% council tax hike Dame Harriett Baldwin confronts the Prime Minister directly over his Minister’s decision to give permission to Worcestershire County Council to hike council tax by 9%. Dame Harriett Baldwin (West Worcestershire) (Con) Q9. The Prime Minister says that he is concerned about the cost of living, so can he explain why he allowed his Local Government Minister to give permission to Reform-led Worcestershire county council to inflict that 9% council tax hike on my constituents? (908559) The Prime Minister (Keir Starmer) Reform has to take responsibility for its decisions, but its Members have moved out. Hansard Dame Harriett Baldwin MP House of Commons Westminster London, SW1A 0AA Tel: 01684 585165 Email: harriett.baldwin.mp@parliament.uk Contact Malvern Hills : Alfrick and Leigh, Baldwin, Broadheath, Chase, Dyson Perrins, Hallow, Kempsey, Lindridge, Link, Longdon, Martley, Morton, Pickersleigh, Priory, Ripple, Teme Valley, Tenbury, Upton and Hanley, Wells, West, Woodbury. Wychavon : Bredon, Eckington, Elmley Castle and Somerville, Pershore Data Protection and Privacy Policy Cookies Official Portrait Photo by UK Parliament Attribution 3.0 Unported (CC BY 3.0) licence .
